sourceMovie.title: The Contractor
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Contractor (2022) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tarik Saleh's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Wick
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Contractor excels in action, John Wick (2014) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Sicario
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Sicario (2015) is the natural next step after The Contractor: same action DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Contractor who respond to its action will find The Transporter (2002) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Mile 22
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Contractor fans keep landing on Mile 22 (2018) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Sum of All Fears
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Sum of All Fears draws from the same action well as The Contractor, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2002 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: A Working Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A Working Man (2025) captures the action appeal of The Contractor without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts A Working Man at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Land of Bad
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of The Contractor — that tense edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Land of Bad (2024). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Harry Brown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Harry Brown (2009) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps The Contractor rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Ballerina
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ballerina (2023) is the kind of film The Contractor fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Nighthawks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Nighthawks shares The Contractor's gift for action — the 1981 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Contractor?
faq.answer: Try John Wick, Sicario, The Transporter—each shares DNA with The Contractor while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Contractor?
faq.answer: The Contractor (2022) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Contractor worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
